body.title: [triage] Minimum nexus write-path in the DS architect loop (write orientation answers, enrich next prefix)
body.description:
**⛔ FAIL — KICKED BACK TO DESIGN 2026-07-13 (Akien: 'those should get sent back to the design step as FAIL for learning from and fixing').**

**THIS TICKET ENTERED SPRINT WITH NO INTENTION.** Under D-intentions-are-the-root-2026-07-13 and proof-on-close, THE INTENTION IS THE PROPERTY MUTATION-RED BREAKS. With no intention there is **no property to break**, so this ticket **cannot be proven as it stands** — it could only ever close hollow, or `shipped-unproven` for a reason that is not the real one.

**IT IS NOT DEFECTIVE WORK — IT IS INCOMPLETE DESIGN.** So the honest disposition is BACK TO DESIGN, not a backfilled intention. CC drafting the missing intention would have **papered over the real defect**: 150 tickets cleared the design step without an intention, which is a **gate hole**, not 150 individual oversights. Per CLAUDE.md, the nuisance stays VISIBLE so it drives finding the fix — never papered over, which sucks silently forever (CP4).

**CP2 — FAIL = FURTHER ADVANCE IN LEARNING.** This kickback IS the learning signal. The fix is upstream: the triage→sprint transition must reject a missing, empty, or degenerate (`TBD`/`unknown`) intention (T-sprint-tickets-with-no-intention-cannot-be-proven).

**TO RE-ENTER SPRINT:** state the intention as a present-tense contract — *'I intend that X, because Y'* — where X names a property a hollow build would violate. Akien declares it (`provenance_class: declared`); a machine-extracted one is a RATCHET, not a SPEC (D-provenance-is-the-safety-property-2026-07-13).

---

**Problem:** the nexus write-side is structurally absent (Fable, CC-verified) — no question ever moves out of the LLM in the coding loop because nothing writes answers back. This ticket builds the minimum-viable nexus write-path (the provable BUILD; the replay experiment that measures it is T-nexus-replay-experiment).

**Proposed shape:** (1) a minimal flat nexus store — `(question_normal_form, answer, falsifier, ticket_id, created_at)`, EXACT/structural keys, NO fuzzy matching. (2) Wire it into the DS ARCHITECT loop: every architect Read/Bash orientation answer is WRITTEN keyed by `(path, content_hash, question_kind)`; each subsequent orientation whose key matches gets the stored answer ENRICHED into its prefix.

**Affected files:** new nexus store module (Postgres or flat-file — ⛔ NO SQLITE); architect_editor.py + agentic_loop.py (write on orientation answer, enrich prefix).

**⚠️ HIGH-inertia pre-approval (Akien, 2026-07-05):** additive touch to the inference design center (architect_editor.py, agentic_loop.py) APPROVED — a write on orientation answers + a prefix-enrich, not a rewrite.

**Design rules:** ⛔ NO SQLITE; EXACT/structural keys only — keep fuzzy matching OUT (pattern_intercept's Jaccard is the silent-wrongness machine); store the `falsifier` column from record 1 (impossible retrofit later) — NOTE: for (path, content_hash) keys invalidation is FREE (changed file → different key → miss), so falsifier is stored for future question-classes, not wired to invalidation machinery yet; log the write crossing.

**Scope boundary:** the store + the DS-architect write+enrich ONLY. NOT: the replay harness/experiment (separate ticket), reference frames, fuzzy identity, invalidation firing, the editor loop.

**Completion criteria:** an architect orientation answer is written to the nexus and retrievable by exact key; a later orientation whose key matches gets that stored answer injected into its prefix.

**Test plan:** hermetic — write answer A for key K, then a later orientation matching K has A injected into its prefix; a non-matching key does not. PROOF NODE: after a nexus write, a subsequent matching orientation's prefix contains the prior answer, where without the write it does not. red→green via proof_emitter.

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set

**RE-SCOPED UP 2026-07-07 (Fable synthesis, D-aider-port-to-nexus-writepath-2026-07-07 — this ticket stays owned by D-compiled-inference-proof-program-2026-07-05, extended not re-spawned):** the nexus row is the FULL triple `fingerprint(build-packet) -> plan-text -> verdict`, not just orientation answers. Add the PLAN-AS-IR row: written at architect-DONE keyed by fingerprint(build-packet); verdict-stamped at ticket close via the existing corpus_verdict join (verdict from T-aider-port-verdict-gate). Keep the orientation-answer rows as the SAME table's second question_kind. A warm hit on fingerprint(packet) returns the cached plan and SKIPS the architect (first/biggest starve-curve point); if the plan carries literal SEARCH/REPLACE blocks (T-aider-port-editor-block-contract), a warm hit can skip the editor too and go straight to the deterministic applier — whose apply-FAILURE on a warm plan IS the falsifier firing. Still EXACT/structural keys, still Postgres/flat-file (NO SQLITE). First warm plan-hit observable via T-nexus-replay-experiment.
body.fail_reason: entered sprint with no intention — cannot be proven; design incomplete
